title: Markdown Basics
description: Learn to format text documents with Markdown, a simple and readable markup language used everywhere from GitHub to note-taking apps.

lessons.title: Headings
lessons.description:
Markdown uses hash symbols # to create headings. One # creates the largest heading (h1), two ## creates a smaller heading (h2), and so on up to six levels.

# Main Title
## Section
### Subsection
lessons.task: Create a main heading by typing <kbd># Hello</kbd>

validations.message: Start with <kbd>#</kbd> followed by a space and your heading text

lessons.title: Heading Levels
lessons.description: Use more <kbd>#</kbd> symbols for smaller headings. <kbd>##</kbd> creates an h2, <kbd>###</kbd> an h3. This creates a clear document structure with visual hierarchy.
lessons.task: Create an h2 heading with <kbd>## About</kbd> followed by an h3 heading with <kbd>### Details</kbd>

validations.message: Add <kbd>### Details</kbd> for the h3 heading

lessons.title: Bold Text
lessons.description: Wrap text in double asterisks ** or double underscores __ to make it bold. This emphasizes important words or phrases.
lessons.task: Make the word <kbd>important</kbd> bold by wrapping it with <kbd>**</kbd>

lessons.title: Italic Text
lessons.description: Wrap text in single asterisks * or single underscores _ to make it italic. Use this for subtle emphasis or titles of works.
lessons.task: Make the word <kbd>elegant</kbd> italic by wrapping it with <kbd>*</kbd>

validations.message: Use single asterisks for italic, not double

lessons.title: Bullet Lists
lessons.description: Create bullet lists using <kbd>-</kbd>, <kbd>*</kbd>, or <kbd>+</kbd> at the start of each line. Each item goes on its own line.
lessons.task: Create a bullet list with three items: <kbd>Apple</kbd>, <kbd>Banana</kbd>, <kbd>Cherry</kbd>

validations.message: Start with a dash, asterisk, or plus followed by <kbd>Apple</kbd>

lessons.title: Numbered Lists
lessons.description: Create numbered lists by starting lines with <kbd>1.</kbd>, <kbd>2.</kbd>, etc. Markdown automatically numbers them in sequence.
lessons.task: Create a numbered list: <kbd>Wake up</kbd>, <kbd>Eat breakfast</kbd>, <kbd>Start coding</kbd>
lessons.solution:
1. Wake up
2. Eat breakfast
3. Start coding

validations.message: Start with a number and period: <kbd>1. Wake up</kbd>
Add <kbd>Eat breakfast</kbd> as a numbered item
Add <kbd>Start coding</kbd> as a numbered item

lessons.title: Links
lessons.description: Create links with <kbd>[text](url)</kbd>. The text in brackets is what readers see; the URL in parentheses is where they go when clicked.
lessons.task: Create a link that shows <kbd>Google</kbd> and goes to <kbd>https://google.com</kbd>

lessons.title: Inline Code
lessons.description: Wrap text in backticks <kbd>`</kbd> to format it as code. This is useful for variable names, commands, or short code snippets in your text.
lessons.task: Format <kbd>npm install</kbd> as inline code using backticks
lessons.initialCode: Run npm install to install dependencies.
